apoe.explanation: One copy of the APOE e4 allele. Associated with approximately 3x increased risk of late-onset Alzheimer's disease compared to e3/e3. Also linked to elevated LDL cholesterol and cardiovascular risk. Protective factors include exercise, Mediterranean diet, cognitive engagement, and adequate sleep.

variants.notes: APOE e4 is the strongest common genetic risk factor for late-onset Alzheimer's disease. One copy confers ~3x risk; two copies ~12x risk. Risk is modifiable through lifestyle factors.
CC genotype at rs7412 excludes APOE e2 allele. Combined with rs429358 CT, confirms e3/e4 diplotype.
Near APOE/APOC1 cluster. Tag SNP for APOE e4 haplotype. Independently associated with lipid levels.
CR1 encodes complement receptor 1, involved in amyloid-beta clearance. Risk allele may impair amyloid removal from the brain.
PICALM involved in clathrin-mediated endocytosis and APP trafficking. Modest effect size.
SLC24A4 encodes a sodium/potassium/calcium exchanger. Mechanism in AD not fully elucidated.
9p21 locus is the strongest common genetic risk factor for CAD. Homozygous risk genotype. Affects CDKN2B antisense RNA regulation of cell cycle genes in vascular smooth muscle.
Second independent signal at 9p21. Strong LD with rs4977574. Homozygous risk significantly elevates CAD risk.
SORT1 variant affects hepatic VLDL secretion. Functional studies confirm causal role in LDL metabolism. T allele associated with lower LDL — this is actually protective.
LPA variants are the strongest genetic determinants of Lp(a) levels. Elevated Lp(a) is an independent causal risk factor for CAD and aortic stenosis. Consider Lp(a) testing.
PCSK9 is a validated drug target for LDL lowering. This common variant has modest effect on LDL levels.
Second LPA signal. Contributes to elevated Lp(a) independently of rs10455872. Additive effect on cardiovascular risk.
TCF7L2 is the strongest common genetic risk factor for T2D. Affects incretin signaling and pancreatic beta-cell function. Risk is modifiable through weight management and physical activity.
Pro12Ala variant in PPARG. Pro allele (C) associated with slightly increased T2D risk. PPARG is the target of thiazolidinedione insulin sensitizers.
E23K variant in KCNJ11. Affects ATP-sensitive potassium channel in pancreatic beta cells. Sulfonylurea drug target.
SLC30A8 encodes zinc transporter ZnT8 in pancreatic beta cells. Risk allele affects zinc transport and insulin crystallization.
FTO variant affects IRX3/IRX5 expression in adipocytes, influencing thermogenesis. Each risk allele associated with ~1.1 kg higher body weight. Effect is attenuated by physical activity.
CHRNA3/CHRNA5 nicotinic receptor cluster. T allele associated with heavier smoking, nicotine dependence, and increased lung cancer risk in smokers.
C282Y mutation in HFE. Heterozygous carrier — unlikely to develop clinical hemochromatosis but may have mildly elevated iron. Monitor ferritin if symptomatic.
H63D variant in HFE. Compound heterozygote with C282Y has ~1% risk of iron overload. Mild impact alone.
Y402H variant in complement factor H. Major genetic risk factor for AMD. C allele impairs complement regulation in the retina. Risk increases significantly after age 55.
ARMS2/HTRA1 locus is the second major AMD risk locus alongside CFH. Combined risk from both loci is multiplicative.
Complement C3 variant. Part of the complement cascade implicated in AMD pathogenesis. Complement inhibitor therapies are FDA-approved for geographic atrophy.
C677T variant reduces MTHFR enzyme activity by ~35% in heterozygotes. May affect homocysteine levels, folate metabolism, and methylation capacity. Supplementation with methylfolate may be beneficial.
A1298C has milder effect than C677T alone. Compound heterozygosity (C677T + A1298C) may have additive effects on enzyme activity.
Val158Met variant. Heterozygote has intermediate COMT enzyme activity. Met (A) allele = slower dopamine breakdown, potentially better working memory but increased stress sensitivity.
Val66Met variant affects activity-dependent BDNF secretion. Met carriers may have reduced hippocampal volume and altered stress response. Exercise increases BDNF regardless of genotype.
Regulates LCT (lactase) gene expression. T allele = lactase persistence (can digest dairy). CT = likely some lactase persistence but may have reduced tolerance with age.
CYP1A2*1F. AC genotype = intermediate caffeine metabolizer. Slow metabolizers may have increased cardiovascular risk from high coffee intake. Consider limiting to 1-2 cups/day.

CYP2C19*2 loss-of-function allele. Heterozygous = intermediate metabolizer. Reduced activation of clopidogrel (Plavix). CPIC guidelines recommend alternative antiplatelet (prasugrel, ticagrelor) if indicated.
CYP2D6*4 allele. Intermediate metabolizer phenotype. Affects metabolism of codeine, tramadol, tamoxifen, many antidepressants. May need dose adjustments per CPIC guidelines.
Ile105Val in GSTP1. Val allele has reduced glutathione S-transferase activity. May affect platinum-based chemotherapy response and oxidative stress handling.
CYP2C9*2 allele. Intermediate metabolizer for warfarin. Requires lower warfarin dose. CPIC/DPWG guidelines recommend pharmacogenomic dosing algorithms.
VKORC1 -1639G>A. T allele associated with lower warfarin dose requirement. Combined with CYP2C9 status, enables pharmacogenomic dosing.
HLA-DQ2.5 haplotype tag SNP. Major genetic risk factor for celiac disease. ~95% of celiac patients carry HLA-DQ2 or DQ8. However, ~30% of the general population also carries these haplotypes.

variants.condition: Multiple autoimmune diseases (RA, T1D, SLE, thyroiditis)
variants.notes: R620W variant in PTPN22 (LYP phosphatase). Pleiotropic autoimmune risk allele affecting T-cell and B-cell signaling. Associated with rheumatoid arthritis, type 1 diabetes, SLE, and Hashimoto's thyroiditis.

variants.condition: Celiac disease / autoimmune risk / blood pressure
variants.notes: Pleiotropic variant affecting immune regulation and hematopoiesis. Associated with celiac disease, type 1 diabetes, and blood pressure regulation.
Tag SNP for HLA-DRB1 shared epitope alleles. Major genetic risk factor for seropositive rheumatoid arthritis.
IL-2 receptor alpha chain variant. Affects T-regulatory cell function. Associated with MS and T1D.
Enhancer region upstream of MYC oncogene. Homozygous risk genotype. Affects MYC expression in colorectal tissue. Recommend standard colorectal cancer screening starting at age 45.
Common BRCA1 missense variant. Moderate increase in breast cancer risk. Not as high-penetrance as truncating BRCA1 mutations. Consider enhanced screening.
Common BRCA1 polymorphism. Low clinical significance per ClinVar consensus. Likely benign.
TOX3/TNRC9 locus. One of the most significant breast cancer GWAS loci. Mechanism involves chromatin regulation.
8q24 region near MYC. Multiple independent cancer risk signals in this region for prostate, breast, and colorectal cancer.
TERT (telomerase reverse transcriptase) variant. Affects telomere maintenance. Associated with risk of multiple cancer types including lung, bladder, and glioma.
Variant in microRNA-196a2. Affects miRNA processing and target gene regulation. Modest association with several cancer types.
Pro72Arg polymorphism in TP53. Both alleles have different functional properties. Pro (C) = better at inducing cell cycle arrest; Arg (G) = better at inducing apoptosis. Context-dependent effects.

variants.condition: Vitamin D receptor function / bone density
variants.notes: FokI variant in VDR. T allele produces shorter, less active VDR protein. May affect calcium absorption and bone mineral density. Ensure adequate vitamin D intake.
TaqI variant in VDR. Associated with bone mineral density variation and osteoporosis susceptibility.

variants.condition: Lower vitamin D binding protein / vitamin D levels
variants.notes: GC (vitamin D binding protein) variant. A allele associated with lower circulating 25(OH)D levels. May need higher vitamin D supplementation.
TNF-308 promoter variant. A allele increases TNF-alpha transcription. Associated with rheumatoid arthritis, IBD, psoriasis, and sepsis susceptibility.
IL-10 promoter variant. T allele associated with lower anti-inflammatory IL-10 production. May contribute to sustained inflammatory responses.
Second independent signal at TCF7L2. In LD with rs7903146 but provides additive risk information.
STAT4 variant affects Th1 and Th17 cell differentiation. Associated with RA and systemic lupus erythematosus.
PITX2 locus on 4q25. Strongest common genetic risk factor for atrial fibrillation. PITX2 is a transcription factor essential for left atrium development.
Second independent signal at PITX2 locus. Additive AF risk with rs2200733.
ZFHX3 (ATBF1) variant. Second major AF susceptibility locus after PITX2.
9p21 locus — distinct from CAD signal at same locus. Affects pancreatic beta-cell proliferation via CDKN2A (p16INK4a) regulation.
FADS1 variant affects desaturase enzyme activity. C allele associated with higher conversion of ALA to EPA/DHA. Affects optimal omega-3 supplementation strategy.

variants.condition: Non-secretor status / B12 levels / gut microbiome
variants.notes: FUT2 secretor status affects blood type antigen expression in gut mucosa. Non-secretors have different gut microbiome composition and may have lower B12 levels but are resistant to norovirus.
SLCO1B1*5 variant. Reduced hepatic uptake of statins. Heterozygous carriers have ~4.5x increased risk of simvastatin myopathy. CPIC recommends lower statin dose or alternative statin.
IFNL3 (IL28B) CC genotype. Favorable response to interferon-based hepatitis C treatment. Also associated with higher spontaneous HCV clearance. CC is the favorable genotype.
I148M variant in PNPLA3. Major genetic risk factor for NAFLD, NASH, and hepatic fibrosis. Risk amplified by obesity and alcohol consumption.
GCKR (glucokinase regulatory protein) variant. Affects hepatic glucose and lipid metabolism. T allele associated with higher triglycerides but lower fasting glucose.
Tag SNP for HLA-B*57:01. Carriers must NOT take abacavir (HIV medication) due to severe hypersensitivity reaction risk. HLA-B*57:01 testing is standard before abacavir prescription.
Taq1A variant near DRD2. T allele associated with reduced D2 receptor density. Implicated in reward processing and addiction susceptibility, though effect sizes are modest.
I22M variant in MTRR. G allele reduces enzyme activity involved in B12-dependent methylation. Combined with MTHFR variants, may affect homocysteine levels.
THADA variant. Associated with T2D in GWAS. May affect apoptosis in pancreatic beta cells.
IL-6 promoter variant. C allele associated with higher IL-6 production. May affect systemic inflammation, CRP levels, and cardiovascular risk.
TT genotype at IFNL3 locus. Favorable for HCV clearance and treatment response. Consistent with rs12979860 CC genotype.
IL-10 promoter variant. G allele associated with higher IL-10 production, which is generally anti-inflammatory. May be protective against some autoimmune conditions.
GC (VDBP) variant affecting vitamin D binding protein isoform. Influences bioavailable 25(OH)D levels.
IGF2BP2 variant. Affects insulin-like growth factor 2 mRNA binding protein 2. Involved in pancreatic beta-cell development.
T300A variant in ATG16L1. Affects autophagy in Paneth cells. Major genetic risk factor for Crohn's disease.
Additional 9p21 signal. In partial LD with rs4977574. Contributes to 9p21 haplotype effect on CAD risk.
TGF-beta1 codon 10 variant. Affects TGF-beta production. Implicated in fibrosis susceptibility and immune regulation.
BCL11A is a key regulator of hemoglobin switching. Variants affect fetal hemoglobin (HbF) persistence. Higher HbF is protective in sickle cell disease and beta-thalassemia.
TMPRSS6 (matriptase-2) variant. A allele associated with lower serum iron and hemoglobin. May predispose to iron-deficiency anemia.

variants.condition: Red hair / fair skin / melanoma risk
variants.notes: MC1R R151C variant. Major determinant of red hair and fair skin phenotype. Carriers have increased melanoma risk independent of UV exposure due to pheomelanin production.
SLC45A2 L374F variant. C allele associated with lighter skin pigmentation. Contributes to UV sensitivity and melanoma susceptibility in light-skinned individuals.
BRCA2 variant. AA genotype = no risk allele present. Normal BRCA2 function expected.
HbS variant. Heterozygous = sickle cell trait carrier. Generally asymptomatic but may have complications at extreme altitude or dehydration. Confers malaria resistance. Important for family planning.
ABCB1 (P-glycoprotein/MDR1) variant. Affects drug efflux transport. May influence bioavailability of many medications including cyclosporine, digoxin, and HIV protease inhibitors.
5-HT2A receptor promoter variant. May influence SSRI antidepressant response and side effects. Evidence is mixed but frequently reported in pharmacogenomic panels.
COMT Val158Met also affects catechol estrogen metabolism. Met carriers may have slower clearance of catechol estrogens, potentially relevant to estrogen-sensitive conditions.
Melatonin receptor 1B variant. G allele associated with higher fasting glucose and increased gestational diabetes risk. May affect circadian rhythm interaction with glucose metabolism.
Sp1 binding site variant in COL1A1 (collagen type I). A allele associated with reduced bone mineral density and increased fracture risk.
APOE rs7412 CC confirms non-e2 carrier status. APOE e4 carriers tend to have shorter lifespan, while e2 carriers have modest longevity advantage.
FOXO3 is one of the few replicated longevity genes across multiple populations. G allele associated with increased lifespan. FOXO3 regulates stress resistance, apoptosis, and metabolism.
Associated with estimated glomerular filtration rate (eGFR). May affect kidney function trajectories.
UMOD (uromodulin) variant. Major genetic determinant of CKD risk and kidney function. Uromodulin is the most abundant protein in urine.

pathways.name: Coronary Artery Disease / Atherosclerosis

variants.notes: 9p21 locus.
Second 9p21 signal.
LPA variant.
SORT1 variant.
9p21 signal.

pathways.assessment: High convergence of coronary artery disease risk variants. Two homozygous risk genotypes at 9p21 locus plus elevated Lp(a) signal create a significantly elevated CAD risk profile.
actions: Request coronary artery calcium (CAC) score
Comprehensive lipid panel including Lp(a)
Consider statin therapy discussion with physician
Lifestyle: Mediterranean diet, regular aerobic exercise, smoking avoidance

pathways.name: Type 2 Diabetes / Metabolic Syndrome

variants.notes: TCF7L2.
PPARG.
KCNJ11.
FTO.
CDKN2A/B.

pathways.assessment: Elevated metabolic risk profile with multiple T2D susceptibility variants including TCF7L2 (strongest common risk locus) and one homozygous risk genotype.
actions: Annual HbA1c and fasting glucose testing
Maintain healthy BMI through diet and exercise
Monitor triglycerides and metabolic markers
Consider Mediterranean or low-glycemic diet

pathways.name: Macular Degeneration / Vision

variants.notes: CFH Y402H.
ARMS2.
C3 variant.

pathways.assessment: Significant AMD risk from combined CFH and ARMS2 risk alleles. Both major AMD loci are positive, creating multiplicative risk especially after age 55.
actions: Annual comprehensive eye exam starting at age 50
AREDS2 supplementation discussion with ophthalmologist
UV protection (sunglasses)
No smoking — strongest modifiable AMD risk factor
Monitor with Amsler grid self-testing

pathways.name: Autoimmune / Inflammatory

variants.notes: HLA-DQ2.5 tag.
PTPN22.
ATG16L1.

pathways.assessment: Multiple autoimmune susceptibility variants present. HLA-DQ2.5 (celiac disease), PTPN22 (pan-autoimmune), and TNF promoter variants indicate heightened immune reactivity.
actions: Celiac screening (tTG-IgA) if GI symptoms develop
Monitor for autoimmune symptoms
Maintain gut health through diverse fiber intake
Consider gluten sensitivity testing

pathways.name: Pharmacogenomics

variants.notes: CYP2C19*2.
CYP2D6*4.
CYP2C9*2.
VKORC1.
SLCO1B1*5.

pathways.assessment: Multiple actionable pharmacogenomic variants. CYP2C19*2, CYP2D6*4, CYP2C9*2, VKORC1, SLCO1B1*5, and HLA-B*57:01 all have FDA-labeled drug interactions.
actions: Share pharmacogenomic results with all prescribing physicians
Avoid clopidogrel — use prasugrel or ticagrelor if antiplatelet needed
Avoid abacavir
Use pharmacogenomic warfarin dosing calculator if warfarin is needed
Use lower statin doses or rosuvastatin (less SLCO1B1-dependent)
Dose-adjust CYP2D6 substrates per CPIC guidelines

pathways.name: Methylation / Folate Metabolism

variants.notes: MTHFR C677T.
MTHFR A1298C.
MTRR.

pathways.assessment: Compound heterozygous MTHFR (C677T + A1298C) with additional MTRR variant. May have moderately reduced methylation capacity. Monitor homocysteine.
actions: Check serum homocysteine level
Consider methylfolate (5-MTHF) supplementation instead of folic acid
Ensure adequate B12 and B6 intake
Leafy greens and folate-rich diet

actionItems.title: Share pharmacogenomic results with all prescribers
actionItems.detail: You carry actionable pharmacogenomic variants (CYP2C19*2, CYP2D6*4, CYP2C9*2, VKORC1, SLCO1B1*5, HLA-B*57:01) that affect how you metabolize common medications. Ensure all healthcare providers are aware. Avoid clopidogrel and abacavir.

actionItems.title: Coronary artery calcium (CAC) score
actionItems.detail: Homozygous 9p21 risk genotype plus elevated Lp(a) genetics create a significantly elevated CAD risk profile. A CAC score will help stratify your 10-year cardiovascular risk and guide statin/aspirin decisions.

actionItems.title: Comprehensive lipid panel with Lp(a)
actionItems.detail: LPA variants (rs10455872, rs3798220) suggest genetically elevated Lp(a). Lp(a) is an independent causal risk factor for CAD and aortic stenosis not captured by standard lipid panels.

actionItems.title: Annual eye exams starting at 50
actionItems.detail: Risk alleles at both major AMD loci (CFH and ARMS2) create elevated macular degeneration risk. Early detection is critical as anti-VEGF therapies can prevent vision loss if started early.

actionItems.title: Annual HbA1c and metabolic panel
actionItems.detail: Multiple T2D susceptibility variants including TCF7L2 and FTO indicate elevated metabolic risk. Regular monitoring enables early intervention.

actionItems.title: Mediterranean diet and regular exercise
actionItems.detail: Your cardiovascular, metabolic, and neurological risk variants are all modifiable through lifestyle. Mediterranean diet and 150+ minutes/week of moderate exercise have the strongest evidence for risk reduction.

actionItems.title: Methylfolate and B-vitamin supplementation
actionItems.detail: Compound heterozygous MTHFR (C677T + A1298C) plus MTRR variant suggest reduced methylation capacity. Consider methylfolate (5-MTHF) rather than folic acid, plus adequate B12 and B6.

actionItems.title: Celiac screening if symptomatic
actionItems.detail: HLA-DQ2.5 carrier. While ~30% of the population carries this haplotype, it is necessary (though not sufficient) for celiac disease. Screen with tTG-IgA if GI symptoms develop.

actionItems.title: Check serum homocysteine
actionItems.detail: MTHFR compound heterozygosity may elevate homocysteine levels. Elevated homocysteine is an independent cardiovascular risk factor that is easily treatable with B-vitamin supplementation.

actionItems.title: Iron studies awareness
actionItems.detail: Compound HFE heterozygote (C282Y/H63D). Very low risk (~1%) of clinical hemochromatosis but may have mildly elevated ferritin. Worth checking if fatigue or joint pain develop.
